commit.message:
feat: Group Context Primitive — sliding window for group chat awareness

Root cause of context fumble: proactive responses were stateless (zero
conversation history), recentContext was always '' (TODO never implemented),
and phantom interactions meant the LLM didn't know what Jarvis already said.

New primitive (group-context.js):
- Sliding window of last 30 messages per group, timestamped
- Tracks both human messages AND Jarvis's own responses
- Injected into system prompt as explicit context block
- Provides recent context to proactive intelligence triage
- bufferAssistantMessage() prevents phantom interactions by recording
  proactive responses in conversation history with correct role

commit.message:
localStorage-first leads: 0 KV reads on page reload

Leads are now persisted in localStorage after the first fetch.
- Page refresh: loads from localStorage instantly, 0 KV reads
- Filters (score, classification, type, stage, urgency): applied client-side, 0 KV reads
- On mount: polls stats (5 KV reads) → only fetches from KV if new leads exist
- Dismiss: updates localStorage immediately, no re-fetch
- Full KV fetch only happens: first ever visit, or when scan adds new leads

commit.message:
refactor: Complete flow-based architecture redesign

Shows step-by-step data flow through the system:
1. Signup Flow - form → validation → DB → email → JWT
2. Onboarding Flow - session → Claude extraction → slot storage → embeddings
3. Matching Flow - 7-step algorithm with formula breakdown
4. Dashboard Flow - match viewing → explanation generation → decision
5. Feedback Loop - how decisions adjust embeddings for future matches

Each step shows:
- What triggers it
- What data flows in/out
- Where data is stored (Redis/DynamoDB/Supabase)
- Code file:line references

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add restart tool to Lean MCP server, fix zombie processes

- Add `mcp__lean__restart` tool: kills lake serve, clears all state,
  starts fresh instance. Useful when imports are stale after rebuilds.
- Fix zombie processes: store Child in ServeProcess instead of
  std::mem::forget, call child.wait() in Drop after SIGKILL to reap.
- Add mcp__lean__restart to allowed permissions.

commit.message:
fix: scope push notifications by tenant to prevent cross-tenant leakage (#498)

## Summary

- Push subscriptions were stored per-user with no tenant scoping,
causing notifications to leak across portals (e.g., a consumer job
completion appearing as "from vocalstar.nomadkaraoke.com")
- Added `tenant_id` field to push subscriptions and filter by tenant
when sending notifications
- Legacy subscriptions (pre-existing, no tenant_id) continue working for
consumer portal

## Changes

**Backend:**
- `PushSubscription` model: added `tenant_id` field
- `PushNotificationService.send_push()`: filters subscriptions by tenant
- `send_blocking_notification()` / `send_completion_notification()`:
extract tenant from job
- `add_subscription()`: stores tenant_id on new subscriptions
- Push subscribe API: accepts `tenant_id` from request body or tenant
middleware
- `JobManager._send_push_notification()`: passes job's tenant_id

**Frontend:**
- `subscribePush()`: sends `tenant_id` parameter
- `usePushNotifications` hook: gets tenant context from `useTenant()`
and passes when subscribing

## Test plan

- [x] 73 tests passing (11 new tenant isolation tests + updates to
existing)
- [x] `TestTenantIsolation`: consumer↔tenant filtering, legacy
migration, empty string normalization
- [x] `TestPushNotificationTenantIsolation` in tenant_separation.py:
end-to-end isolation tests
- [x] Route tests verify tenant_id passthrough and normalization
- [x] CodeRabbit: clean review, no findings

<!-- @coderabbitai ignore -->
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pi Zero 2 W: real hardware SSH, HTTP fetch, USB gadget, MVM self-compilation

Complete bare-metal stack on real Pi Zero 2 W hardware over USB CDC-ECM.
DWC2 in device/gadget mode with interrupt-driven RX, SSH server with
Ed25519/X25519/ChaCha20, HTTP client and server, actor-based cooperative
scheduling. Boots via rpiboot (no SD card), redeploys via UART bootloader.

MVM compiler enhanced for self-compilation: compiles its own 759 functions
to architecture-independent bytecode. Prelude provides MVM-compilable
implementations of CL library functions (hash tables, sort, mapcar, etc.).
New opcodes (FUNCALL, MULTIPLE-VALUE-BIND, FLET) and macros (LDB, LIST,
VECTOR, multi-place SETF) support the full compiler source.

Key changes:
- DWC2 USB gadget driver with CDC-ECM Ethernet (Full Speed, PIO mode)
- Interrupt-driven USB RX via ISR at 0x1000 (4-slot ring buffer)
- Pre-computed crypto at boot to avoid NETDEV WATCHDOG (5s timeout)
- USB keepalive polling in crypto hot loops (x25519, ed25519, fe-sq-iter)
- HTTP/1.0 client with DNS resolution and URL parsing
- HTTP/1.0 server on port 80
- Gateway MAC learning from incoming IP packets (all net-actor-main variants)
- Uninitialized RAM fixes throughout (make-array doesn't zero on real hardware)
- MVM prelude.lisp: hash tables, sort, mapcar, gensym, symbol-value, format stubs
- MVM compiler: flet/labels, funcall, LDB, LIST, VECTOR, multi-place SETF
- All 7 translators updated with new opcodes
- Boot scripts for Pi Zero 2 W (USB boot, SD card, OTP fuse programming)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: pod creation 500 error — image_position NOT NULL constraint violation

The INSERT explicitly passed null for image_position on non-text_image pods,
but the column has a NOT NULL constraint. Changed to pass 'left' as default.
Also added try/catch to POST handler and updated init.sql with missing columns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add clouds and atmosphere to terrestrial moons

Terrestrial moons (habitable, with green land) now have:
- Animated cloud layer: multi-octave simplex noise, slowly drifting,
  lit by starlight. Density 30-55%, white/blue-white color.
- Atmosphere rim glow: blue-tinted Fresnel edge glow that's visible
  even slightly on the dark side (atmospheric scattering).
- Higher geometry resolution (4 subdivisions vs 3) for smoother rim.

This makes it visually obvious that a terrestrial moon has an
atmosphere, distinguishing it from airless rocky/ice types.

commit.message:
Persist crash history + leaderboard, seed fake results on first load

- Crash history saved to localStorage, survives refresh
- First-time visitors see seeded history with big multipliers (12.8x, 24.6x, 7.2x) to create FOMO

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(lora): replace radio.available() with DIO0 polling

radio.available() only works in interrupt-driven mode; in polling mode
it always returns 0, making receive non-functional. Correct approach
for RadioLib SX127x polling: check DIO0 pin directly — it goes HIGH
when a packet is received (configured by startReceive()) and LOW after
IRQ flags are cleared, preventing stale TX data from being read.

feat: module knowledge transfer (export/import) and guidance.md injection

## sinain-koog — module_manager.py
- export: package any module as a portable .sinain-module.json bundle
  (manifest + patterns.md + guidance.md + registry entry)
- import: install a bundle into a workspace; --activate auto-registers it;
  --force overwrites existing modules
- guidance: read/set/clear per-module guidance.md from the CLI
- Triple store hooks: ingest on activate, retract on suspend

## sinain-hud-plugin
- collectModuleGuidance(): reads guidance.md from all active modules (sorted
  by priority desc) and injects a [MODULE GUIDANCE] block into agent context
- Transferred module attribution: if effective playbook contains imported
  modules, prepend [KNOWLEDGE TRANSFER] notice so agent cites origins
- generateEffectivePlaybook(): adds "> *[Transferred knowledge: ...]*" label
  for imported modules using manifest.importedAt
- guidance.md added to DEPLOY_ONCE set (deploy-once semantics like patterns.md)

## sinain-koog — other
- insight_synthesizer.py: prompt instructs attribution of transferred patterns
- triple_extractor.py / triple_ingest.py: handle guidance triples from modules
- tests/test_module_management.py: 300+ line test suite for export/import/guidance

## modules
- claude-code-workflow: new active module (priority 60) — patterns and guidance
  for Claude Code workflow integration
- module-registry.json: claude-code-workflow registered as active

## demo
- demo_knowledge_transfer.sh: end-to-end shell demo of the transfer flow
  (expert export → novice import → KG ingest → verification)

feat: use Range for frame annotations to show solid bars in Encord editor

Instead of individual frame dots (sampled every INFERENCE_STRIDE frames),
annotations now render as contiguous bars:

- Classifications: Range(min_frame, max_frame) per unique answer group,
  so each detected label spans from first to last observed frame.
- Objects: Range(frame_idx, frame_idx + INFERENCE_STRIDE - 1) per detection,
  so adjacent sampled frames form a connected bar.

Fix line detection and remove broken word boundary detection

Line detection: added smoothing, adaptive threshold, and auto-splitting of
overly tall regions that merged multiple text lines into one. This fixes the
"huge multi-line sections assigned to one word" bug.

Removed pixel-based word boundary detection (detectWordsInLine) — it was
fundamentally unreliable for handwriting. Words now store null coordinates
and the editor shows line crops + text-only word cards.

commit.message:
feat(sbd): v1.1.0 — quote-aware segmentation, verse detection, and hybrid consolidation

Major improvements to sentence boundary detection:

- Text mode classification (text_modes.py): two-pass verse detection with
  relaxed threshold for long song lines (56-75 chars), dialogue attribution
  guard to prevent false-positive verse on rapid dialogue exchanges
- Quote-aware merge: local paragraph-scoped quote depth tracking merges
  Punkt over-splits inside quoted discourse (82% reduction in Alice)
- Exclamation continuation rule: merges "thump!", "splash!", "Alas!" with
  lowercase continuations
- Hybrid verse consolidation: short verse blocks (<=8 lines) become single
  sentences; longer blocks (epic cantos) keep internal boundaries. Captures
  Alice's songs without collapsing the Iliad.
- Export schema: sentence type field, logical key ordering, sort_keys=False
- span_tokenize replaces brittle str.find span reconstruction
- 100 tests (unit + integration), all passing

Alice delta vs reference: +10 (down from +31). Iliad/Inferno preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: the-derple-dex
repository.description: Technical blog and project showcase

commit.message:
security: harden external privacy issues E2, E3, E4

E2 — Invite token endpoint (app/api/invite/route.ts)
  Add strictLimiter rate check (5 req/min per IP). The endpoint was
  entirely unprotected, allowing brute-force enumeration of invite
  tokens to harvest email addresses, names, and family names.

E3 — Export signed URLs (app/api/export/route.ts)
  Reduce signed URL lifetime from 3600s (1 hour) to 300s (5 minutes)
  in both the GET and POST handlers. A fresh URL is generated on every
  GET request so this is transparent to users, but a copied/cached URL
  now expires in 5 minutes instead of sitting live for an hour.

E4 — Notifications batch emails (app/api/notifications/route.ts)
  Convert the two grace-period reminder/enforcement emails from a single
  Resend call with to:[array] to individual per-recipient sends (matching
  the pattern already used by birthday and weekly-digest emails). Sending
  a multi-recipient array in one API call exposes the full family
  membership list to the email provider.
